title: 10 best herbal teas for period cramps
intro_desc: Herbal teas for period cramps are a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with menstrual cramps. These teas contain plant-based ingredients that help to relax the uterine muscles, reduce inflammation, and increase blood flow, thereby providing relief from cramping. Examples of herbal teas that help with period cramps include chamomile, peppermint, and ginger tea, which have been shown to ease pain and discomfort. By drinking these teas regularly, women can experience a significant reduction in cramp severity and frequency, allowing them to live more comfortably throughout their periods.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with period cramps because it contains compounds that have a natural anti-inflammatory effect, which can help to reduce uterine contractions and alleviate cramping. The tea's flavonoids and isoflavones also have estrogen-like properties, which can help to balance hormonal fluctuations that may contribute to menstrual cramp severity. Additionally, the calming properties of licorice root can promote relaxation and reduce overall discomfort associated with PMS symptoms.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce prostaglandins' ability to cause uterine contractions and alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice tea have been shown to relax smooth muscle tissue, including the uterine muscles that contract during menstruation, reducing cramping and discomfort.
Isoliquiritigenin: This compound has been found to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones responsible for causing menstrual cramps, thereby providing relief from period pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and ease menstrual cramp pain.
Barks: Licorice barks have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe cramping and discomfort during menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier or your own cultivated source, ensuring quality control measures.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured dried glycyrrhiza glabra root with the boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on desired strength and flavor profile, then strain carefully into a cup.
Serve the warm tea immediately, without adding any sweeteners or creamers, to fully appreciate its natural sweetness and herbal properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with period cramps because of its natural ability to relax muscles, reduce inflammation, and ease digestive discomfort. The menthol in peppermint oil has a calming effect on the uterine muscles, which contract during menstruation, thereby relieving cramping and spasms. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and pain. By sipping on a warm cup of herbal peppermint tea, women can find relief from the discomfort associated with their periods.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol helps to relax smooth muscle spasms, including those in the uterus, reducing menstrual cramp intensity and duration.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and ease pelvic pain associated with period cramps.
Volatile oils (including menthone and limonene): These compounds have a natural analgesic effect, helping to numb the pain receptors in the uterus and reduce menstrual cramp severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate menstrual cramp pain and reduce inflammation in the uterus.
Buds: The buds of the peppermint plant contain a high concentration of menthol, which has natural analgesic properties that help relieve menstrual cramp discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality peppermint leaves from your garden or store-bought supply.
Rinse the dried peppermint leaves thoroughly under cold running water for 30 seconds.
Combine one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves with one cup boiling filtered water in a heat-resistant teapot.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes, then strain and discard the solids immediately.
Drink the tea hot or allow it to cool completely, consuming within 20-30 minutes before your period starts.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn teas helps with period cramps because it contains flavonoids, such as quercetin and rutin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and discomfort. The tea also contains magnesium, a mineral that relaxes the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and cramping. Additionally, hawthorn's sedative effects can promote relaxation, helping to alleviate anxiety and stress that often accompanies menstrual cramps. As a natural and gentle remedy, hawthorn tea provides relief without harsh chemicals or medications, making it an effective and soothing choice for women experiencing period cramps.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Hawthorn tea's flavonoids, such as quercetin and rutin, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause menstrual cramps.
Phenolic acids: The phenolic acids in hawthorn tea, including gallic acid and caffeic acid, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate period pain and discomfort.
Oleanolic acid: Hawthorn tea contains oleanolic acid, a triterpenoid that has been shown to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles, which can help ease menstrual cramps and spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which can help relax uterine muscles and reduce menstrual cramp severity.
Barks: The barks contain quercetin, a potent anti-inflammatory agent that can help ease period discomfort and reduce pain.
Fruits: Hawthorn fruits are rich in anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that may help alleviate menstrual cramps by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers or 3-4 teaspoons of fresh material.
Measure one teaspoon of the prepared herb and place it in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Heat water to a rolling boil and pour 8 ounces over the herb, allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using the infuser or by pouring it through a fine-mesh sieve, discarding the solids.
Let the tea cool slightly before consuming, aiming for 1-2 cups per day throughout the duration of menses.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry teas helps with period cramps because it contains a unique combination of compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The tea's flavonoids, specifically quercetin, work to reduce prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions leading to cramping. Additionally, the tea's omega-3 fatty acids help to relax uterine muscles, further alleviating discomfort. As a result, highbush cranberry teas can provide relief from menstrual cramps, making it a natural and effective way to manage period pain.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: Highbush cranberry tea contains anthocyanins, which are powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps.
Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid present in highbush cranberry tea that has natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to ease period cramp discomfort.
Vasodilatory compounds: The tea contains vasodilatory compounds like rutin and hesperidin, which help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing menstrual cramp severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the highbush cranberry plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing menstrual cramp pain and discomfort.
Seeds: The seeds of the plant contain fatty acids and antioxidants, which help to relax uterine muscles and reduce spasms associated with period cramps.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried cramp bark herb per cup of boiling water.
Steep the herb in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea leaves and discard them after preparation is complete.
Combine the herbal tea with lemon juice or honey as desired for taste and effectiveness.
Allow the tea to cool before drinking it within 30 minutes of steeping.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica teas helps with period cramps because of its natural ability to relax the uterine muscles, reducing inflammation and pain. The tea's active compounds, such as angelic acid and sesquiterpenes, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which in turn alleviates muscle spasms and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps. Additionally, herbal angelica teas may also help regulate hormone imbalances, further contributing to its analgesic properties and ability to provide relief from period cramps.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Angelica tea has been shown to relax uterine muscles and reduce spasms, providing relief from menstrual cramp discomfort.
Pain-relieving properties: The active compound, ligustilide, in angelica tea has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate pain associated with period cramps.
Antioxidant properties: Angelica tea's high antioxidant content helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to menstrual discomfort and cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Angelica archangelica are used to make tea because they contain a high concentration of compounds that help to relax uterine muscles and reduce menstrual cramp severity.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of the plant, are used in teas for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pelvic pain and discomfort associated with period cramps.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried angelica root per cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured angelica root with 250ml of boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes allowing the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids.
Enjoy the warm tea immediately, ideally within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with period cramps because it contains a natural compound called anethole, which has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. This allows it to effectively soothe and relax the uterine muscles, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps. Additionally, fennel's warming and calming effects can help to ease digestive issues that often accompany period cramps, promoting a sense of relief and comfort throughout the duration of your period.
remedy_properties: Estrogen-regulating flavonoids: Fennel tea's flavonoids have been shown to regulate estrogen levels, which can help alleviate symptoms of PMS and menstrual cramps.
Spasmolytic compounds: Fennel's essential oils contain compounds like anethole, which has a natural ability to relax uterine muscles and reduce menstrual cramp severity.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel tea's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to period cramps and other PMS symptoms.

Seeds: Containing anethole, a natural anti-inflammatory compound, fennel seeds soothe uterine spasms and relieve cramping.
remedy_recipe: Measure out two teaspoons of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves for every eight ounces of water.
Bring the water to a boil and then reduce the heat to allow it simmer for seven minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup.
Discard the solids and let the tea steep for an additional five minutes before serving hot.
Enjoy the tea as soon as possible, ideally within thirty minutes of preparation for optimal relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with period cramps because its calming properties ease muscle tension, reducing discomfort and pain associated with menstrual cramps. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also help to relax uterine muscles, further alleviating cramp symptoms. remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried lavender flowers from a reliable source.
Combine the lavender flowers with 4 cups of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or fine-mesh sieve and discard solids.
Enjoy the soothing tea warm or at room temperature within an hour of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea teas helps with period cramps because they contain natural compounds that can help relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramp severity. Bring the mixture to a boil and then reduce heat and simmer for ten minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together.
Strain the tea into a cup and add honey or lemon to taste, serving hot and enjoying immediately after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with period cramps because its natural anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and cramping. Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before drinking the liquid.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried valeriana officinalis root or two teaspoons of fresh leaves.

remedy_recipe: Measure 2 tablespoons of dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis into a clean glass jar.

Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or individual cups.

Measure 1 teaspoon of dried hypericum perforatum herbs for every 8 ounces of boiling water needed for steeping the tea blend.
Combine the measured hypericum perforatum herbs with boiling water in a teapot or infuser and let it steep for 10-15 minutes to release optimal medicinal properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding any solids before serving while still warm for best flavor and efficacy.
Consume the tea within 24 hours of preparation, enjoying two cups throughout the day as needed to help alleviate period cramp discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Motherwort teas helps with period cramps because of its unique ability to relax the uterine muscles, reducing inflammation and spasms that can cause severe discomfort. The tea's rich concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids works to calm the uterus, alleviating cramp severity and frequency. Additionally, motherwort's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that stimulate uterine contractions and exacerbate cramping. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, motherwort teas provide a natural and effective solution for managing period cramps.
remedy_properties: Spasmodic relief: Motherwort's sedative properties can help to ease spasms and cramping associated with menstruation by calming the uterine muscles, allowing for a more comfortable menstrual experience.

Antispasmodic effects: Motherwort contains compounds that have antispasmodic properties, which means it can help to reduce muscle spasms and cramps by relaxing the smooth muscle tissue in the uterus, thereby providing relief from period pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Motherwort's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation in the pelvic area, which is thought to contribute to period cramps and other menstrual discomforts, thus providing additional relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are valued for their ability to soothe and calm uterine spasms, reducing menstrual pain and cramping.
Leaves: The leaves contain bioflavonoids that help to relax the smooth muscles in the uterus, alleviating discomfort and cramping during menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality leonurus cardiaca leaves and stems from trusted sources or online suppliers in quantities sufficient for your needs.
Dry the harvested leaves and stems using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8-12 hours or air-drying them for several days.
Combine one teaspoon of dried leonurus cardiaca with boiling water to make a single serving, let steep for 5-7 minutes and then strain.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature before consumption, which should be within 30-40 minutes of preparation.
Drink two to three cups of leonurus cardiaca tea daily during menstruation to help alleviate period cramps, bloating and other symptoms.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat period cramps with herbal teas is to brew a warm cup of Chamomile tea, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the uterus. Another option is Ginger tea, which has analgesic properties that help reduce pain and discomfort. Additionally, Peppermint tea can be used to relax the muscles and ease cramps, while Raspberry leaf tea helps regulate hormonal balance and alleviate symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to period cramps that are treated with herbal teas are digestive issues, such as bloating and constipation. Teas like peppermint, chamomile, and ginger help soothe the stomach and alleviate discomfort. Additionally, herbal teas may also be used to treat menstrual migraines and other premenstrual symptoms (PMS) like anxiety and mood swings. The anti-inflammatory properties of these teas can provide relief from cramp-like sensations and promote overall well-being.
